package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "crypto/md5"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for weak_hash_for_integrity: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `md5.New()`, `sha1.New()`, `md5.Sum()`, `sha1.Sum()` used for integrity checks, checksums, or MAC operations (not just `weak_crypto` import-level detection)
type CaseWeakHashForIntegrityDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseWeakHashForIntegrityModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseWeakHashForIntegrityRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseWeakHashForIntegrityModel
}

type CaseWeakHashForIntegrityService struct {
    repo *CaseWeakHashForIntegrityRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseWeakHashForIntegrityAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveWeakHashForIntegrity(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseWeakHashForIntegrityDTO) error {
    focus := "weak_hash_for_integrity"
    _ = focus
    _ = md5.Sum([]byte(input))
    weak_hash_integrity_positive := map[string]int{"weak": 1, "hash": 2, "integrity": 3}
    weak_hash_integrity_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    weak_positive_0 := weak_hash_integrity_positive["weak"] + len(input)
    _ = weak_positive_0
    hash_positive_1 := map[string]string{"hash": input}
    _ = hash_positive_1
    integrity_positive_2 := weak_hash_integrity_positive["integrity"] + len(input)
    _ = integrity_positive_2
    return nil
}
